claims.flip_evidence_text: the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the “Notice Period”) to the effect that the Company Board has ... (C) resolved to ... terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii)
claims.notes: Contract requires at least three calendar days' prior written notice before termination to enter an Alternative Acquisition Agreement; initial notice period is not shortened by subsequent two-Business-Day revision notices.

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
5.3. No Solicitation. <omitted> (ii) if the Company has received a bona fide written Acquisition Proposal that did not result from a breach of this Section 5.3 and that the Company Board has concluded in good faith (after consultation with its financial advisor and outside legal counsel) is a Superior Proposal, then the Company Board may (A) effect a Company Board Recommendation Change with respect to such Superior Proposal or (B) authorize the Company to terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 8.1(h) to enter into an Alternative Acquisition Agreement with respect to such Superior Proposal, in each case if and only if: (1) the Company Board determines in good faith (after consultation with its financial advisor and outside legal counsel) that the failure to do so would be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties pursuant to applicable Law; and (2) (i) the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the “Notice Period”) to the effect that the Company Board has (A) received a bona fide written Acquisition Proposal that has not been withdrawn; (B) concluded in good faith that such Acquisition Proposal constitutes a Superior Proposal; and (C) resolved to effect a Company Board Recommendation Change or to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii), which notice will describe the basis for such Company Board Recommendation Change or termination, including the identity of the Person or “group” of Persons making such Acquisition Proposal, the material terms and conditions of such Acquisition Proposal and copies of all relevant documents relating to such Acquisition Proposal; and (ii) prior to effecting such Company Board Recommendation Change or termination, the Company and its Representatives, during the Notice Period, have (1) negotiated with Parent and its Representatives in good faith (to the extent that Parent requests in writing to so negotiate) to make such adjustments to the terms and conditions of this Agreement so that such Acquisition Proposal would cease to constitute a Superior Proposal, and (2) provided Parent and its Representatives with an opportunity to make a presentation to the Company Board regarding this Agreement and any adjustments with respect thereto (to the extent that Parent requests to make such a presentation), it being understood that (a) in the event of any material revisions to such Acquisition Proposal, the Company will be required to deliver a new written notice to Parent and to comply with the requirements of this Section 5.3(d)(ii)(2) with respect to such new written notice (with the “Notice Period” in respect of such new -64- written notice being two Business Days, provided that such new notice shall in no event shorten the original three calendar day notice period); and (b) the Company Board, at the end of the Notice Period (after consultation with its financial advisor and outside legal counsel), must have in good faith reaffirmed its determination that such bona fide written Acquisition Proposal is a Superior Proposal. (Pages 67-69) 5.3. No Solicitation. <omitted> (ii) if the Company has received a bona fide written Acquisition <omitted> the Company Board <omitted> may <omitted> (B) authorize the Company to terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 8.1(h) to enter into an Alternative Acquisition Agreement with respect to such Superior Proposal, <omitted> if: <omitted> (2) (i) the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the “Notice Period”) to the effect that the Company Board has <omitted> (B) concluded in good faith that such Acquisition Proposal constitutes a Superior Proposal; and (C) resolved to effect a Company Board Recommendation Change or to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii), <omitted> ; and (ii) prior to effecting such <omitted> termination, the Company and its Representatives, during the Notice Period, have (1) negotiated with Parent and its Representatives in good faith <omitted> to make such adjustments to the terms and conditions of this Agreement so that such Acquisition Proposal would cease to constitute a Superior Proposal, and (2) provided Parent and its Representatives with an opportunity to make a presentation to the Company Board regarding this Agreement and any adjustments with respect thereto (Pages 67-69) 5.3. and (2) (i) the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the “Notice Period”) to the effect that the Company Board has (A) received a bona fide written Acquisition Proposal that has not been withdrawn; message.reasoning:
The user wants me to evaluate a single claim against a contract excerpt, a question, and a model answer (Llama answer).

Let me break down the components:
1. Contract excerpt: Section 5.3(d)(ii) about No Solicitation. It states that the Company Board may authorize termination to enter into an Alternative Acquisition Agreement with respect to a Superior Proposal if and only if certain conditions are met. One condition is: "(2) (i) the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the 'Notice Period') to the effect that the Company Board has ... (C) resolved to ... terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii)..."

2. Question: "Initial matching rights period (FTR)-Answer" - This seems to be asking about the initial matching rights period or the notice period required.

3. Llama answer: "The Company must provide Parent with at least three calendar days' prior written notice before terminating the Agreement to enter into an Alternative Acquisition Agreement with respect to a Superior Proposal."

4. Canonical claim: claim-322d4ee5c1c1: "The Company must provide Parent with at least three calendar days' prior written notice before terminating the Agreement to enter into an Alternative Acquisition Agreement with respect to a Superior Proposal."

Now I need to evaluate:
- correctness_label: Is the claim true, false, or partially true based on the contract?
- load_bearing_label: Is this claim load-bearing? (i.e., does it support the answer to the question?)
- flip_evidence_text: If load-bearing, provide the evidence text that would flip the claim's truth value (i.e., the specific text in the contract that supports or refutes it).
- notes: Optional short note.

Correctness analysis:
The contract explicitly states: "the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the 'Notice Period') to the effect that the Company Board has ... resolved to ... terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii)..."

So the claim that the Company must provide Parent with at least three calendar days' prior written notice before terminating the Agreement to enter into an Alternative Acquisition Agreement with respect to a Superior Proposal is TRUE.

Load-bearing analysis:
The question asks about the "Initial matching rights period (FTR)-Answer". The Llama answer provides the notice period requirement. The claim directly restates the Llama answer. Since the claim is essentially the answer to the question, it is load-bearing. Wait, I need to be careful. The task says "load_bearing_label: yes or no". A claim is load-bearing if it is central to the answer. Here the claim IS the answer (or the main substance of it). So yes, it is load-bearing.

Flip evidence text:
If load-bearing, I need to provide the evidence text from the contract that would flip the claim's truth value. Since the claim is true, the flip evidence would be the text that establishes its truth (i.e., the supporting text). Wait, the instruction says "flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string". In the context of these annotation tasks, "flip evidence" usually means the specific text in the source that determines the truth value of the claim (i.e., if you changed this text, the claim would flip). Or it could mean the evidence that supports/refutes the claim. Let me think about the typical usage in legal annotation tasks.

Actually, looking at the term "flip_evidence_text", it likely refers to the specific contractual text that bears on the claim's truth value - the text that if flipped (changed) would change the label. So I should quote the relevant passage from the contract that supports the true label. If the claim is true, the flip evidence is the text that makes it true. If false, the text that makes it false.

The relevant text is: "the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the 'Notice Period') to the effect that the Company Board has ... resolved to ... terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii)..."

I should provide a concise excerpt.

Let me check if there's any nuance. The contract says "at least three (3) calendar days before (the 'Notice Period')". The claim says "at least three calendar days' prior written notice before terminating". This matches. The contract also mentions that in case of material revisions, a new notice with a 2 Business Day period is required, but the initial period is 3 calendar days. The question asks for "Initial matching rights period (FTR)-Answer", so the 3 calendar days is correct for the initial period.

Therefore:
- correctness_label: true
- load_bearing_label: yes
- flip_evidence_text: "the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the 'Notice Period') to the effect that the Company Board has ... resolved to ... terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii)"
- notes: empty or brief confirmation.

Wait, I need to make sure I format the output correctly. The user wants for each claim:
- correctness_label
- load_bearing_label
- flip_evidence_text
- notes

Since there's only one claim (claim-322d4ee5c1c1), I should provide the evaluation for it.

Let me double-check the exact wording in the contract to quote accurately. The full relevant sentence is:
"(2) (i) the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the “Notice Period”) to the effect that the Company Board has (A) received a bona fide written Acquisition Proposal that has not been withdrawn; (B) concluded in good faith that such Acquisition Proposal constitutes a Superior Proposal; and (C) resolved to effect a Company Board Recommendation Change or to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii), which notice will describe the basis for such Company Board Recommendation Change or termination..."

For the flip evidence, I can use a focused excerpt:
"the Company has provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the 'Notice Period') to the effect that the Company Board has ... resolved to ... terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii)"

Or maybe just:
"provided prior written notice to Parent at least three (3) calendar days before (the 'Notice Period') ... resolved to ... terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 5.3(d)(ii)"

I think that's sufficient.

One more check: Is the claim partially true? The contract says 3 calendar days, and the claim says 3 calendar days. It's fully true. The contract also mentions a 2 Business Day period for revised proposals, but the claim is about the initial notice, which is correctly stated as 3 calendar days. So true.

Load-bearing: The question is about the initial matching rights period. The claim directly answers this. So yes, load-bearing.

Notes: I can leave it empty or add a brief note. I'll leave it empty or just say "Initial notice period is three calendar days."

Actually, looking at the prompt again: "For each claim return: ... notes: optional short note". I'll provide a short note.

Final check on flip_evidence_text: The prompt says "required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string". So I must provide it.

Let me format the output clearly.
